entry.title: Deactivation of finance master data objects in S/4 HANA cloud private edition - Cost centers
entry.content:
Hi All,

Finance master data is a well governed metadata object as its driven by financial reporting requirements. GL chart of accounts are defined as per the external reporting requirements of US-GAPP, IFRS etc. Cost centers are used to drive internal management reporting to understand the expenses and costs incurred by various products/functions while profit centers drive management reporting of overall profitability of  products and services. Profit centers also drive the revenue /cost of revenue numbers for reporting.

Over a period of time organizations business models evolve and certain teams, services are wind down, merged etc. However most of the times the associated metadata elements are still active and lying in your ERP landscape dormant. Over a period of  ~5 years the organization would have gone through multiple changes and you will have lot of cost centers, profit centers, WBS & internal order which are active but never used. 

IMPORTANCE OF DEACTIVATION:

Business perspective:

1. It avoids accidental booking of invoices, expense reports or even manual journals to obsolete cost objects.

2. If a planner has 20 cost centers under him but only 5 are being actively used, he might have to manually put 0 or NA comments for rest 15 cost centers in every forecast cycle.

3. It safe guards allocation runs from allocating costs to outdated organizational unit.

Systems Perspective:

1. Every time a financial analyst/Accounting controller pulls a report, the runtime of the these reports are faster as its querying a smaller subset of data.

2.Reconciliation of master data across different systems finance, CRM, procurement , HR is easier and any discrepancy can be identified efficiently.

3. It helps to maintain a better data quality score as we need to focus only on actively used objects rather than all active objects in the system.

Now lets talk about how we achieve the deactivation of cost center which was previously being used to track Opex ( both Payroll as well as non payroll ) 

CRITERIA FOR DEACTIVATION FOR COST CENTERS

The deactivation trigger can come from :

1. Deactivation due to changes in organizational structure.

2. Dormant cost centers for a certain period of time defined by the finance team of an organization.

PRE-REQUISTIES FOR DEACTIVATION

1. Any cost center should not have any recurring activity on BS or P&L accounts. Use the GL line items display app to check for activity. This should be done for all ledgers.

2. Fixed Assets: Check for fixed assets which are tagged to the cost center. If there are any active assets which have NBV value and a depreciation schedule then asset master has to be adjusted and a balance reclass of the NBV needs to be performed.

3. Accruals:  There might be prepaids tagged to a cost center, e.g.; payment for service done at once but accrued over 12 months, leases etc. In such cases if the prepaids are still being amortized then adjust the account assignment  ( cost center ) in the accrual engine and also reclass the remaining value on the balance sheet to the new cost center.

4. Headcount/Payroll: If a particular team or department was wound down, then the existing headcounts needs to be transferred from the existing cost center to new cost center. In some cases if a cost center has 0 headcount doesn't mean that payroll activity will cease to exist. There might be cases where there are pending liabilities which the company has to recover from employee who has left from the cost center or the company has net payables to employees or some pending taxation. It's best to align with payroll teams to take their confirmation.

5. Purchase orders/Service entry sheets:  All purchase orders needs to be closed. This will ensure there are no invoices raised against a PO once the CC is closed.  Similarly all the service entry sheets either have to be closed or has to be updated with new account assignment to avoid errors.

6. WBS elements -> Any WBS element which is settling to cost center needs to be also reviewed and updated to ensure the settlement runs don't result in any errors.

7. Balance sheet balances: Any balance sheet balance has to be cleared/reclassed to alternate cost centers with same account assignments in all currencies ( company code currency, transaction currency , group currency ) . This is to ensure that there is no impact to the revaluation and currency translation processes during month ends.

8 Budgeting/Forecasting : Before a cost center is closed, ensure the budget is moved to central cost pool or to a alternate cost center to ensure that budget is not blocked and can be utilized. The forecasting should also be updated to reflect the forecast values in new cost centers.

What MDG has done in S/4 HANA 2023 & beyond?

MDG now has provided the capability to perform remote checks for cost center from a hub system to your central transactional S/4 system to ensure that before you submit a blocking request you know that it doesn't have any lingering balances. More details can be found in note 

3517066 - MDG-F: Validation of changes in (remote or local) S/4 HANA systems

However this doesn't cover all the pre-requisites which i have mentioned above.

Operational challenges: 

 In any large organization, the processes across finance, supply chain, HR, CRM are running on various platforms both on-prem and cloud. To give a simple example SAP S/4 HANA will be the single source of truth for all financials, however the upstream processes like CRM could be running on SaaS platform, HR processes might be running on some non-SAP 3rd party tool, similarly planning and budgeting could be running on some in-house developed tool. With so many systems even though SAP MDG is used maintaining the data lineage is a challenge  as there will be a multistep integrations. Also there might be some systems which are not at all integrated to operational issues and technical challenges.

CONCLUSION

Proactive deactivation of dormant cost objects is essential for maintaining a lean, high-performing financial ecosystem that prevents reporting errors and operational friction. While modern tools like MDG-F provide a foundation, a truly robust data lifecycle requires seamless cross-functional alignment to ensure long-term integrity across complex, multi-platform landscapes.

*This is my own experience of working on this topic, and not a comprehensive list of all the checks one needs to perform. However this might be a good starting point if you are looking to deactivate cost centers.*

SAP Master Data Governance  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Finance 

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model: What's New as of 2026-05-04?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

Welcome to this month’s What’s New blog post for SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud – Standard Model (SAP PaPM Cloud SM). We’re excited to share the latest updates designed to give you more control, flexibility, and clarity in how you work with our platform. This release focuses on enhancing everyday workflows—from how you stay informed, to how you interact with AI, manage your data, and understand model compatibility—so you can work more confidently and efficiently.

Read on to see what’s new!

 

User Preferences | Notifications

An enhancement for notifications in the SAP PaPM Cloud SM is now available, introducing team-based notifications from the Environment Access screen, where users can view environments, manage their access type (Public or Private), and maintain team assignments for private environments.

To receive these notifications, users must have notifications enabled in User Preferences → Notifications, with either In-App or Email  Notifications selected (at least one option must be active).

If enabled, users will receive team-related notifications by email, in-app, or both. Users will be alerted in the following scenarios:

  • When editing a Private environment and assigning a team, all users who belong to that team will receive a notification.
  • When a team is unassigned from a Private environment, users who are part of that team will be notified.
  • When an environment is changed from Private to Public, users receive a notification about the access change.
  • When an environment is changed from Public to Private, notifications are sent only to users who are part of the teams assigned to that environment.

To learn more about Environment Access Screen and roles that grant edit in Environment Access, please refer to the Application Help.

 

Settings | Global Notification Settings

A new Global Notification Settings feature is now available in the SAP PaPM Cloud SM, enabling tenant Administrators to centrally manage notification behavior across all Users.

From the Settings screen, administrators can configure notification preferences for different notification types, such as: activity changes, private environment changes, process changes, scheduled jobs, and team changes.

For each notification type, the following options are available:

  • Always send – notifications are always delivered to users and cannot be disabled.
  • Allow users to manage settings – users can control their own notification preferences in User Preferences.
  • Never send – notifications are completely disabled for all users.

These settings apply globally and override individual user preferences where enforced, ensuring consistent and controlled notification behavior across the tenant.

For more details on configuration and usage scenarios, you may refer to the following blog post, SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model: Global Notification Settings.

 

Settings | Joule AI Enhancements

Joule is now available on the Report Management screen, even when the SAP AI Core service is not configured in the Joule AI Settings. When the required settings are not maintained, the system automatically enables the built-in Joule capability included in the SAP PaPM Cloud SM.

This built-in setup is intended for light use with predefined daily usage limits per user. The system informs users when they are close to reaching these limits or have exceeded them. After the daily limit is reached, Joule remains available, but responses may be slower due to automatic throttling. 

With this update, users can start using Joule immediately, making it suitable for occasional questions and simple ad hoc analysis. For scenarios that require frequent or daily use, integrating SAP AI Core via SAP BTP is recommended to provide access to the full version of Joule, with higher throughput and extended usage capabilities. 

⚠️ Note for SAP HANA Cloud with BYOD (Application) Subscription

When using the SAP PaPM Cloud SM with a standalone SAP HANA Cloud database as part of the BYOD (Application) subscription, Natural Language Processing (NLP) must be enabled in SAP HANA Cloud to use Joule in Report Management.  

More information about this requirement and how to enable NLP is available in the SAP Help Portal: Use Joule Without AI Core Service in Standard Model Reporting.

 

Content Management | Convert Unsupported Functions at Runtime

Some imported environments can contain legacy function types such as Model BW and Model RDL, which were previously supported in SAP Profitability and Performance Management On-Premise, but are not supported in the current SAP PaPM Cloud SM function set. 

When importing environments through Content Management in SAP PaPM Cloud SM, unsupported Model RDL and Model BW functions contained in the ZIP file are now automatically converted to Model Table at runtime. 

During the import process, the conversion is recorded in the import message log, which can be reviewed in both the Content Management screen and the Application Monitor. After the import is completed, users can open the environment in the Modeling screen and see that the previously unsupported Model RDL or Model BW functions are now available as Model Table in the diagram.

Once an environment has been imported and the unsupported functions have been converted, exporting that environment again no longer carries the original unsupported function types. As a result, the same unsupported‑function conversion message is not expected to appear again for the already converted content in subsequent imports. 

For more information about this update, see SAP Help Portal.

 

Data Management Screen | Exclude Comments from Query Function Data Upload and Download

For a Query function that is set as Editable, users can manipulate its data by uploading and downloading its data via the Data Management Screen. With this update, to avoid incorrect comment mapping across records or environment, the Comments field values are intentionally excluded during data upload and download  

A query element will be considered as a Comment field when it is tagged as a Comment Key in the Properties Panel. 

When downloading the query data from the Data Management screen, the Comment field or column will be included and visible in the export file but will not have any value. Same as with uploading data, the Comment field will still be required in the import file, however none of its values will be imported or displayed. If any validation is needed, the Comment field will not be visible on the Upload Preview page. 

⚠️ Note: This update is only applicable to Query Functions. Other Model Table behaviors should remain the same. 

For more information about this update, please see our SAP Help Portal for Upload Data and Download Data. 

That’s all for now!

Thank you for taking the time to read—we hope these updates help make your everyday work with SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model even more seamless. Keep an eye out for the next edition of our What’s New series as we continue delivering more enhancements throughout 2026.

Until next time,
Happy modeling! 😊

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Paris : Qui êtes-vous ?
entry.content:
Bonjour à toutes et à tous,

Je me présente : je suis Oula Douayéré Francis, basé en région parisienne.

Mon parcours initial est orienté finance d’entreprise, avec une expérience en environnement finance et une exposition à SAP dans un contexte de transformation et de procédures financières.

Aujourd’hui, je développe progressivement une spécialisation à l’intersection de la finance, des ERP et de la data : SAP Finance / données ERP, Data Engineering, KPIs financiers, automatisation de pipelines, MLOps et IA appliquée à la finance.

Je ne me présente pas encore comme expert SAP senior, mais comme un profil en montée en compétence sérieuse sur l’exploitation des données SAP/ERP pour produire des analyses financières, des tableaux de bord, des contrôles qualité et, à terme, des cas d’usage IA autour de la finance.

Je travaille actuellement sur un projet personnel de plateforme Data Finance multi-ERP : RAW → CANONICAL → SILVER → GOLD, avec des indicateurs comme DSO, DPO, trésorerie, risque crédit, qualité comptable et working capital.

Je rejoins donc cette communauté pour apprendre des profils expérimentés, mieux comprendre les pratiques réelles autour de SAP, SAP Finance, SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud et les intégrations avec les plateformes data modernes.

En dehors du travail, je suis passionné par la data, le football et l’analyse de performance.

Ravi de rejoindre les SAPien.ne.s de Paris et d’apprendre avec vous.

entry.title: Exception handling for actions in SAP BPA
entry.content:
Hi,

A customer would like to be able to manage the response of an action even when it fails (e.g., with status codes 401, 404, etc.).

For example, we need to call an action to create a sales order, but the action may fail because the customer ID does not exist. In this case, we don’t want the entire process to fail. Instead, we would like to handle the error, for instance, by allowing the user to correct the customer ID through a form.

I’ve seen that this capability has been delivered:
https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73554900100800003832&range=FIRST-LAST#;INNO=81C4C671B92A1EDFA2F214564741EB46

Is there any documentation available on this feature? How does it work?

Thank you!

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Re: Workflow Trigger Behavior After Project Approval
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, when a workflow is triggered by changing a project status to “Released,” the approval process must be completed before the project is officially released. Once approved, the project remains in the released status. If the project data is modified afterward, the workflow does not automatically re-trigger unless you explicitly configure new start conditions. To prevent manual release when integrating with an external BPM system, you should restrict status changes via authorization roles and let the BPM system control the release action.
Check this SAP Help document Manage Workflows for Enterprise Project

entry.title: Re: SAP Customizing issue - Person Responsible
entry.content:
Dear @XavierJ,

In Public Cloud this is standard behavior for configuration activity 102966 Determine Person Responsible. This activity is treated as Redo in P / system-specific configuration, so the changes are not recorded in a transport request. Therefore the message is expected and does not indicate a transport issue because Redo in P = Yes

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3290703

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3572476

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2754127

For this type of configuration, SAP Public Cloud transport model requires the setting to be maintained manually in the relevant target tenant. In this case the entries maintained in A8L/100 may be available in A8L/120 depending on the local system setup but they are not transported automatically to A7F/120

So the correct action is to maintain the same Person Responsible configuration directly in A7F/120

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Cost Center Derivation on Non-Cost Element GR/IR Acco...
entry.content: In S/4HANA Public Cloud, GR/IR accounts should not derive cost centers unless inherited from PO account assignment or substitution logic. This is not intended standard behavior. The recommended fix is to use substitution rules to clear the cost center field or adjust PO/account assignment settings to prevent inheritance.

entry.title: Dynamic authorisation with user attributes in SAP CAP and AMS
entry.content:
Introduction: The Business Challenge

Your company just acquired a new business unit. Overnight, 500 users need access to sales data, but only for their division. Traditional role-based access control means creating roles like Sales_Manager_EMEA, Sales_Manager_APAC, Sales_Manager_LATAM, and countless variations. As dimensions multiply (division, region, department, cost center), you face role explosion—hundreds or thousands of roles that don't scale.

There's a better way. With Authorisation Management Service (AMS) and Cloud Identity Services, you define a single policy with a restriction:

Give READ access where division = $user.division

The $user.division pulls from the authenticated user's profile. Authorisation administrators manage policies through the Cloud Identity Services console—no code changes, no deployments. When a sales manager transfers from EMEA to APAC, HR updates the division attribute in the corporate identity system, and access automatically adjusts across all applications. No role re-assignment required.

Understanding the Technical Foundation

Building on recent SAP Community blogs on AMS (here and here), let's focus on attribute-based authorization.

The stack:

  • Cloud Identity Services authenticates users and stores attributes (division, cost center, region)
  • AMS manages and evaluates authorisation policies at runtime
  • CAP applications enforce authorisation using user attributes for dynamic filtering

Traditional RBAC vs. attribute-based:

  • Traditional: Sales_Manager_EMEA, Sales_Manager_APAC, Sales_Manager_LATAM → doesn't scale
  • Attribute-based: where division = $user.division → dynamic, adapts automatically

The runtime flow:

  1. User logs in through corporate IdP (e.g., Microsoft Entra ID)
  2. IAS returns JWT with custom attributes (division, warehouse, etc.)
  3. CAP extracts attributes and makes them available to AMS
  4. Database queries are automatically filtered based on user attributes
  5. User sees only data they're authorised to access

This approach delivers measurable business benefits: significant reduction in "access change" requests to IT teams, and a single source of truth in IAS for access decisions with complete audit trail for compliance.

Implementation Walkthrough: The Complete Picture

Let's walk through the actual implementation:

  1. IAS Configuration: Configure custom attributes in Cloud Identity Services
  2. AMS Schema: Declare available user attributes in the schema
  3. CAP Server Integration: Extract attributes from JWT and map to CAP & AMS user object
  4. Authorisation Policies: Create policies in Cloud Identity Services with attribute-based restrictions

Step 1: Configure Custom Attributes in Cloud Identity Services

Business context: Your organization structures access by division and warehouse. Users should only access data related to their assigned division and warehouse location.

Configuration steps:

  1. Navigate to IAS Admin Console → Applications → Your Application
  2. Under the Trust → Attributes tab, add new attributes:
    • Name: division
    • Source: Cloud Identity Services or the corporate identity provider

Do the same for the warehouse attribute.

Example user attributes:

  • alice@example.com → division: "LOG", warehouse: "MAD"
  • bob@example.com → division: "RET", warehouse: "BCN"

Verify the attribute in JWT:

After authentication, decode the JWT token (e.g., at jwt.io) and you will find:

 
{
	"sub": "alice@example.com",
	"division": "LOG",
	"warehouse": "MAD"
}

This confirms the custom attribute is flowing from IAS into your application's authentication token.

Step 2: Define User Attributes in AMS Schema

Before using $user.division or $user.warehouse in policies, you must declare it in the AMS schema. This tells the AMS runtime what attributes are available for policy evaluation.

Edit ams/dcl/schema.dcl:

 
SCHEMA {
	"$user": {
		groups: String,
		division: String,
		warehouse: String
	}
}

What this does:

  • Declares the structure of user attributes available in policy expressions
  • division and warehouse are now recognized by the DCL compiler
  • Enables autocomplete and validation in policy files

Step 3: Map JWT Token Attributes to AMS Input

The critical piece: AMS needs to extract custom attributes from the JWT token and make them available to policy evaluation. This is where you connect the IAS attributes to the CAP/AMS authorisation context.

Create a custom auth provider in srv/server.js:

 
const cds = require('@sap/cds');
const { amsCapPluginRuntime, IdentityServiceAuthProvider } = require("@sap/ams");

class CustomAuthProvider extends IdentityServiceAuthProvider {
/**
* @Param {import("@sap/xssec").IdentityServiceSecurityContext} securityContext
*/
	getInput(securityContext) {
		const defaultInput = super.getInput(securityContext);
		// Extract custom attribute from JWT token payload
		const division = securityContext.token.payload.division;
		if (division) {
			defaultInput["$user.division"] = division;
		}
		// You can add more attributes here
		const warehouse = securityContext.token.payload.warehouse;
		if (warehouse) {
			defaultInput["$user.warehouse"] = warehouse;
		}
		return defaultInput;
	}
}

// Register the custom auth provider
amsCapPluginRuntime.authProvider.xssecAuthProvider = new CustomAuthProvider(amsCapPluginRuntime.ams);

What this does:

  • Extends AMS's IdentityServiceAuthProvider to intercept token processing
  • Extracts division and warehouse from the JWT payload (e.g.: securityContext.token.payload.division)
  • Maps it to $user.division for use in DCL policies and CAP restrictions
  • Attributes are now available throughout your authorisation logic

Verification: You can log the attribute to confirm it's accessible:

 
// In srv/service.js
this.before('READ', 'Orders', async (req) => {
	console.log('User division:', req.user.attr.division || 'not set');
	console.log('User warehouse:', req.user.attr.warehouse || 'not set');
});

Step 4: Create Authorisation Policies with User Attribute Restrictions

Now that attributes are flowing from IAS → JWT → CAP/AMS context, you can create authorisation policies that reference them.

Cloud Identity Services Authorisation Policies Approach

Navigate to Cloud Identity Services → Applications → Your Application → Authorisation Policies to create policies with attribute-based restrictions.

Creating a DivisionReader policy:

  1. In the Cloud Identity Services admin console, open your application
  2. Navigate to the Authorisation Policies tab
  3. Create a new restriction (e.g., ReaderByDivision) based on an existing restriction (Reader in the example below)
  4. Add a Restriction: division = $user.division
  5. Assign users or groups to this policy

What happens at runtime:

  1. User with division: "LOG" requests GET /orders
  2. AMS evaluates the DivisionReader policy with $user.division = "LOG"
  3. The restriction division = $user.division becomes division = "LOG"
  4. CAP injects WHERE division = 'LOG' into the SQL query
  5. User only sees orders for the Logistics & Distribution division
  6. The same query executed by a user with division: "RET" automatically returns only Retail Operations orders

CAP Service restriction annotations approach

For developers who prefer to define authorisation rules in code (especially during development), you can also reference $user.division directly in CAP service definitions:

 
service OrderService {
	@restrict: [{
		grant: ['READ'],
		to: ['ReadOrders'],
		where: 'division = $user.division'
	}]
	entity Orders as projection on data.Orders;
	@restrict: [{
		grant: ['READ'],
		to: ['ReadInventory'],
		where: 'warehouse = $user.warehouse'
	}]
	entity Inventory as projection on data.Inventory;
}

Benefits and Separation of Concerns

This attribute-based authorisation approach delivers concrete advantages through clear separation of responsibilities:

For Developers: Build the technical foundation once

  • Configure attribute extraction from JWT tokens
  • Declare available attributes in the AMS schema
  • Deploy the application

For Authorisation Administrators: Manage access through the UI

  • Create policies in Cloud Identity Services
  • Define attribute-based restrictions without coding
  • Modify policies as organizational needs change—no deployments required

For HR/Identity Teams: Maintain user attributes as single source of truth

  • Update user profiles when employees change roles or divisions
  • Attributes automatically sync to Cloud Identity Services with Identity Provisioning
  • Access adjusts across all integrated applications

References

Check out the ams-samples-node repository.
CAP Developer Guide: Role Assignment with AMS
AMS documentation

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA CDS View Dependency Navigator
entry.content:
Hello  Uladzislau,

this report is really great for analysis. Though I noticed that V3 CDS Views are not considered in the "where used list".

For example:

define transient view entity ZCO_C_MarginSemTag

provider contract analytical_query

with parameters

...

as projection on I_MargAnlysProdAndSrvcCube

(P_GLAccountHierarchy :$parameters.P_GLAccountHierarchy)

 {

Maybe the reason is that V3 CDS Views don´t use "sqlViewName". Here is an example from I_MargAnlysProdAndSrvcCube which uses:

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'IPROSRVMARGIN'

Screenshots:

So the CDS View Dependency Navigator does not find the V3 CDS Views, but the V1 CDS Views. 

Maybe you find a solution for this problem?

Thank you

Regards

David

 

Edit:

Here is an example with SAP Standard V3 CDS Views in an OnPrem 2023

C_ActlPlnSrvcMargItemQuery

as projection on I_ActlPlnSrvcMargItemCube

While the I_ActlPlnSrvcMargItemCube can be found via your report, the C_ActlPlnSrvcMargItemQuery can´t be found.

entry.title: Re: How to strikeout the Po line item in adobe forms
entry.content:
Hi @Mahil ,
My friend you can absolutely strike through deleted PO line items in an SAP Adobe Form — but Adobe Forms won’t automatically apply strikethrough based on SAP logic. You need to pass a flag from ABAP and then apply a text style (or rich text) in the form. You detect deleted PO items in ABAP (LOEKZ = 'L'), pass a flag to the Adobe form, and then apply strikethrough formatting using either:

Option 1 — Adobe Form Text Field with Script (Recommended)
Use a text field with Rich Text enabled and dynamically wrap the value in <strike>...</strike>.

Option 2 — Use Paragraph Style with Strikethrough
Create a paragraph style in Adobe LiveCycle Designer and apply it conditionally.

Hope the solutions helped you.

entry.title: Re: How to add custom Header fields to FB02/FB03 (Display...
entry.content:
For a header-level custom field in FB02, you should go with a BAdI-based approach (e.g., FI_DOCUMENT_CHANGE) instead of BTEs.

Why because, BTEs mainly support line-item enhancements and are not suited for header UI changes.  BAdI + enhancement framework provides clean, flexible, and future-proof handling for header fields (both UI and logic).
